title: lithocarpus chrysocomus
intro: Lithocarpus chrysocomus, also known as the goldback chinquapin, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family Fagaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and its bark is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bark of the goldback chinquapin has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Lithocarpus chrysocomus is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
